codeReview.summary: The feature adds the expected frontend registry/hook infrastructure and broad test coverage, but two blocking correctness issues remain: node-scoped onConnectionValidate hooks are filtered against the wrong context field and plugin registration is not atomic, allowing partial stale state on mid-registration failure.

issues.description: Node-scoped hook filtering checks only context.nodeType, but OnConnectionValidateContext primarily uses sourceNodeType/targetNodeType. As implemented, per-node onConnectionValidate hooks can be skipped unless callers redundantly set nodeType, which breaks the expected onConnectionValidate hook behavior for node-entry hooks. Evidence: filterHookByNodeType reads only context.nodeType (ab4a44c plugin-registry.ts:106-114), while OnConnectionValidateContext exposes sourceNodeType/targetNodeType (ab4a44c nodes/_types.ts:299-303).

registerPlugin mutates nodesByType and registers hooks incrementally before the plugin is committed to plugins map. If a later node entry fails (e.g., collision/missing definition), earlier node registrations/hooks remain but plugins.set is never reached, leaving orphaned state that unregisterPlugin cannot clean. Evidence: pushRegisteredNode/registerHookCollection inside loop before plugins.set (ab4a44c plugin-registry.ts:195-214).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker procedure is too generic around hook testing and did not force coverage for node-scoped behavior across all hook types; this allowed a node-scoped onConnectionValidate filtering defect to pass feature tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: frontend-worker SKILL.md Step 3 says to test hook dispatch but does not require per-hook-type node-scoped coverage; commit tests cover node-scoped filtering only for onNodeCreate (app/lib/flow/__tests__/plugin-registry.test.ts:101-120 in ab4a44c).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture notes list hook names but not frontend hook context-shape expectations (e.g., connection hooks using source/target node types), which increases risk of incorrect generic hook filtering implementations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md documents hook names at high level but omits context field contracts; mismatch is visible between plugin-registry nodeType-only filter (ab4a44c plugin-registry.ts:111) and OnConnectionValidateContext fields (ab4a44c nodes/_types.ts:299-303).

summary: FAIL — reviewed worker session 22127a4c-3c9d-4474-ba8b-bafd89e15c82 and commit ab4a44c. The feature is close, but it has two blocking defects: incorrect node-scoped filtering for onConnectionValidate hooks and non-atomic plugin registration that can leave stale partial registry state after a failed registration.
